name: El Mirador del Tombo y la Ermita de Corona — circular en el Parque Nacional Picos de Europa
region_intro: Jogging routes around Posada De Valdeón are situated within the Picos de Europa National Park, characterized by rugged limestone peaks, deep gorges, and verdant valleys. The terrain features a mix of paths, beech forests, and meadows, offering varied surfaces for running. Glacial erosion has shaped a landscape of impressive massifs and diverse geological formations. The region provides a natural backdrop for outdoor activities, including trail running. Best jogging routes around Posada De Valdeón The most popular jogging route is Cares Trail: Posada de Valdeón to Cordiñanes, a 3.6 miles (5.8 km) trail that takes about 37 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Valdeón valley and follows a section of the Cares Trail. Another top favourite among local runners is Santa María de Valdeón, Cordiñanes de Valdeón, Posada de Valdeón — circular in the Parque Nacional Picos de Europa, a difficult 18.1 miles (29.1 km) path. This extensive circular route traverses through villages and offers significant elevation changes within the national park. Local runners also love the Refugio de Vegabaño - trail running round trip from Puerto de Panderrueda through the Picos de Europa National Park, a 9.5 miles (15.3 km) trail leading through the Picos de Europa National Park, often completed in about 2 hours 46 minutes. Jogging around Posada De Valdeón is defined by rugged limestone peaks, deep gorges, and lush valleys, with routes offering varied terrain for different ability levels. The routes in Posada De Valdeón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from 5 reviews. More than 700 runners have used komoot to explore Posada De Valdeón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Posada de Valdeón?
A: There are over 15 dedicated running routes around Posada de Valdeón, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels within the Picos de Europa National Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Posada de Valdeón?
A: The terrain is diverse, characterized by rugged limestone peaks, deep gorges, and lush green valleys. You'll find a mix of paths, beech forests, and meadows, shaped by glacial erosion, providing varied surfaces for trail running.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners in Posada de Valdeón?
A: While many routes are challenging, there are moderate options available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1228869142/cares-trail-posada-de-valdeon-to-cordinanes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cares Trail: Posada de Valdeón to Cordiñanes&lt;/a&gt; offers a 5.8 km path with relatively mild elevation changes, making it a good choice for those looking for a less strenuous run.

Q: Are there challenging trail running options in the Valdeón Valley?
A: Yes, Posada de Valdeón is known for its demanding routes. For experienced trail runners, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1229777277/santa-maria-de-valdeon-cordinanes-de-valdeon-posada-de-valdeon-circular-in-the-parque-nacional-picos-de-europa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María de Valdeón, Cordiñanes de Valdeón, Posada de Valdeón — circular in the Parque Nacional Picos de Europa&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 29.1 km circular route with significant elevation changes, traversing through villages and offering a true mountain running experience.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see while running in the area?
A: Many routes offer stunning views. You can pass by the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6051790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cares Gorge (Garganta Divina)&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy panoramic vistas from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/280873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cable Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; (accessible via the Fuente Dé cable car) or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1229000656/el-mirador-del-tombo-y-la-ermita-de-corona-circular-en-el-parque-nacional-picos-de-europa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Mirador del Tombo&lt;/a&gt;, which provides breathtaking views of the surrounding peaks and valleys.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Posada de Valdeón?
A: Absolutely. A notable circular option is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1229777277/santa-maria-de-valdeon-cordinanes-de-valdeon-posada-de-valdeon-circular-in-the-parque-nacional-picos-de-europa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María de Valdeón, Cordiñanes de Valdeón, Posada de Valdeón — circular in the Parque Nacional Picos de Europa&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to experience diverse landscapes without retracing your steps.

Q: What is the best time of year for trail running in Posada de Valdeón?
A: The Picos de Europa National Park is generally best for trail running from late spring through early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is typically more stable, and trails are clear of snow, offering ideal conditions to enjoy the stunning scenery.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Posada de Valdeón?
A: As Posada de Valdeón is located within the Picos de Europa National Park, dogs are generally permitted on trails but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always check specific trail regulations before heading out.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Posada de Valdeón?
A: The running routes in Posada de Valdeón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Reviewers often praise the unparalleled natural beauty, the dramatic mountain landscapes, and the variety of terrain that makes each run a unique adventure.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass through forests or offer shade?
A: Yes, the Valdeón Valley features a mix of landscapes, including beautiful beech forests. The 'Bosques de Valdeón' circular trail, for example, winds through ancient forests, providing shade and a different running experience amidst the trees.

Q: Is there public transport access to the trailheads in Posada de Valdeón?
A: Public transport options within the Picos de Europa National Park can be limited, especially to more remote trailheads. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to access specific starting points for your runs.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Cares River?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38172037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta del Cares Trail – View of the Cares Gorge loop from Caín de Valdeón&lt;/a&gt; offers spectacular views of the Cares River as it carves through the dramatic gorge. A portion of the Cares Trail is also accessible from the Valdeón valley, specifically Caín de Valdeón.
tips: The viewing point was opened on 10 August 1964 and features an iron cross and a stone column with a sculpture of a chamois. There is also an engraving listing all the peaks that can be seen from the site, making it easier to identify them.

Although the exact details of its origin are unclear, it is believed to have been built in the 12th or 13th century. Over the centuries it has been restored and renovated on various occasions to maintain its structure.
